decision: Achieve closed-loop Windows client validation against a real Linux Firecracker nanofused running in WSL2 (which exposes /dev/kvm). Build mount + secret-reference visibility surfaces across daemon/API/CLI/tray to reach feature-complete parity beyond the current mac client flow.
rationale: Operator selected WSL2 Firecracker daemon for closed loop and explicitly opted to also build mount+secret surfaces. Windows cannot host the runtime (config rejects windows GOOS); WSL2 is the only local Linux+KVM path on this machine.

decision: Firecracker backend lacks API exec (CommandExecutor) so nanofuse vm exec 501s, unlike mac apple_container. To reach exec parity for the closed loop, implement firecracker Exec via the system ssh client + the CI image key (no new Go module deps), returning a clean unsupported error when sshd/key absent. Sequenced AFTER mounts/secrets lands.
rationale: Operator bar is 'equal to mac version'; mac closed loop included vm exec. SSH-backed exec gives real parity without adding x/crypto dependency or modifying the guest agent stack.

decision: Do NOT implement firecracker API exec in this pass. Document it as the single backend-capability difference (apple_container supports API exec; firecracker is a bare microVM where exec is via SSH/guest-agent). WINDOWS_RESUME.md does not list exec as a required operator surface; the two documented blockers (mount + secret visibility) are resolved. Generic firecracker exec needs per-VM rootfs SSH-key injection at start, which risks destabilizing the validated boot path for a non-required surface.
rationale: Smallest change satisfying the verified goal; avoid destabilizing working closed loop; honest documentation over fragile image-coupled hack.
